outlook.headline: AI power delivery stays constructive, but breadth is still thin
outlook.summary: Texas Instruments’ read on GPU power-delivery sockets as complex and high-level points to continued strong AI/datacenter demand and supports content growth for power semis. But the signal is not yet broad: ON Semiconductor only asked for architecture clarification, and mention activity is flat with slightly negative momentum versus trend, so the next 1-2 quarters look supportive but not clearly accelerating.

drivers.label: GPU power-delivery complexity signals strong AI demand
drivers.detail: Texas Instruments described GPU power-delivery sockets as complex and high-level, implying more silicon/content intensity in datacenter power.

drivers.label: Datacenter power-semiconductor demand remains healthy
drivers.detail: Texas Instruments’ bullish framing suggests the AI/datacenter buildout is still pulling on power-delivery components.

drivers.label: Limited confirmation from peers
drivers.detail: ON Semiconductor’s latest discussion was only a product-architecture clarification, with no direct demand or supply read to broaden the bullish signal.

drivers.label: Mention trend is flat to slightly down
drivers.detail: Company mentions were essentially unchanged at 0.0x, with momentum -2% versus the trailing average, indicating no clear broadening yet.

risks: More suppliers may continue to offer only technical clarification rather than demand validation.
If mention intensity stays flat, the AI power-delivery thesis may remain company-specific instead of sector-wide.
Any change in GPU power architecture could alter the mix of beneficiaries within power semis.
